title: Complexity, Timing, Profiling, Memory, and Capacity · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent archive team receives a different authorized corpus, platform, language mix, file size, report consumer, terminal width, error policy, and delivery constraint and transfers complexity, timing, profiling, memory, and capacity into a changed correctness-preserving capacity study. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of correctness-preserving capacity study with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, change…
objectives: State workload, input scale, expected complexity, environment, metric, budget, and correctness invariant before measuring.
Use repeated controlled timing, warmup decisions, robust summaries, and noise disclosure rather than one stopwatch value.
Use cProfile or a justified profiler to locate cumulative work before selecting a repair.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Performance Hypothesis
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Bookbot team handles case 7397 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to measure representative time and memory while preserving byte-for-byte correctness; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Performance Hypothesis.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for state workload, input scale, expected complexity, environment, metric, budget, and correctness invariant before measuring. For Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Performance Hypothesis, keep Performance Hypothesis connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-performance-regression-1f3i1p
# Competency: bookbot-performance-regression.
# Case 8899: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def evidence_bookbot_performance_regression_1f3i1p(tokens: int = 10000, elapsed_ms: float = 42.0, peak_kib: int = 640, time_budget_ms: float = 100.0, memory_budget_kib: int = 1024, output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1f3i1p = "1f3i1p-p1i3f1-8899"
    assert evidence_variant_1f3i1p
    evidence_axes_1f3i1p = (
        "corpus1f3i1p",
        "policyp1i3f1",
        "revision8899",
        "command1f3i1p8899",
        "reportp1i3f18899",
        "test88991f3i1p",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1f3i1p)) == 6
    if tokens < 1 or elapsed_ms < 0.0 or peak_kib < 0:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "measurement-shape"
    if elapsed_ms > time_budget_ms:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "time-budget"
    if peak_kib > memory_budget_kib:
        return False, peak_kib, "memory-budget"
    if not output_equal:
        return False, elapsed_ms, "semantic-regression"
    assert elapsed_ms <= time_budget_ms
    return True, round(elapsed_ms, 3), "profiled-capacity"
